qa_pairs.question: What is the contract number for this universal life policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Contract Number: FPS-UL-24-03391

qa_pairs.question: What is the specified face value of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Specified Amount (Face Value): $400,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the planned monthly premium?
qa_pairs.evidence: Planned Premium: $200/month

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum premium payment?
qa_pairs.evidence: Minimum: $75 per payment

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum annual premium subject to tax limitations?
qa_pairs.evidence: Maximum: $50,000 per year (subject to tax limitations)

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed minimum interest rate credited to the account value?
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.2 Guaranteed minimum rate: 2.0% per annum

qa_pairs.question: What is the current credited interest rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: Current rate: 3.5% per annum (subject to change monthly)

qa_pairs.question: What is the monthly administrative expense charge?
qa_pairs.evidence: (b) Administrative expense charge: $8.00/month

qa_pairs.question: What is the premium expense charge percentage deducted when premiums are paid?
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.2 Premiums are allocated to the Account Value after deduction of a 3% premium expense charge.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum partial withdrawal amount?
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.2 Minimum withdrawal: $500

qa_pairs.question: What is the issue date of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Issue Date: March 10, 2024

qa_pairs.question: What is the surrender charge in Year 1 as a percentage of specified amount?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.2 Surrender Charge Schedule (% of Specified Amount): Year 1: 8%

qa_pairs.question: What death benefit option is currently selected on this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Death Benefit Option: Level (Option A)

qa_pairs.question: What is the insured's date of birth?
What company issued this universal life policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: FlexProtect Insurance Solutions

qa_pairs.question: Who is the primary beneficiary of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Primary: Jennifer Ann Wilson (Wife) - 100%

qa_pairs.question: Who are the contingent beneficiaries and their shares?
qa_pairs.answer: Robert Wilson Jr. (Son) at 50% and Emily Rose Wilson (Daughter) at 50%
qa_pairs.evidence: Contingent: Robert Wilson Jr. (Son) - 50% / Emily Rose Wilson (Daughter) - 50%

qa_pairs.question: Under Option B, how is the death benefit calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: Death Benefit equals the Specified Amount plus the Account Value.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.2 OPTION B (Increasing Death Benefit) - Available by Request: Death Benefit = Specified Amount + Account Value

qa_pairs.question: What does the Cash Surrender Value equal?
qa_pairs.answer: Account Value minus Surrender Charge minus Outstanding Loans
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.1 Cash Surrender Value = Account Value - Surrender Charge - Outstanding Loans

qa_pairs.question: How is the Cost of Insurance charge calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: (Specified Amount minus Account Value) multiplied by the Monthly COI Rate divided by 1,000
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.2 Cost of Insurance is calculated as: (Specified Amount - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate / 1,000

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the Account Value is insufficient to cover monthly deductions?
qa_pairs.answer: The policy enters a 61-day grace period during which the policy continues.
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.4 If Account Value falls below the amount needed to pay monthly charges, the policy will enter a 61-day grace period.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if premium is not received within 61 days when the account value is insufficient?
qa_pairs.evidence: 10.2 If sufficient premium is not received within 61 days, policy lapses.

qa_pairs.question: What is paid upon lapse if account value exceeds surrender charges?
qa_pairs.answer: Any Account Value in excess of surrender charges will be paid to the policy owner automatically.
qa_pairs.evidence: 10.3 Upon lapse, any Account Value in excess of surrender charges will be paid to you automatically.

qa_pairs.question: What is paid if the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the Account Value, without interest.
qa_pairs.evidence: 9.1 SUICIDE EXCLUSION: If insured dies by suicide within 24 months of issue date, we pay only the Account Value, without interest.

qa_pairs.question: What fee applies when changing the death benefit option if coverage is being increased?
qa_pairs.answer: A $50 processing fee is required, plus evidence of insurability.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.3 You may change death benefit options subject to: (a) Evidence of insurability if increasing coverage (b) Payment of a $50 processing fee

qa_pairs.question: After what year do partial withdrawal charges cease?
qa_pairs.answer: After Year 10 there is no charge.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.3 Withdrawal charges: Years 1-10: $25 per withdrawal / Years 11+: No charge

qa_pairs.question: Under what conditions can the insurer contest this policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The insurer may contest for material misrepresentation within 2 years of the issue date while the insured is living.
qa_pairs.evidence: 9.2 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue date while insured is living.

qa_pairs.question: What is the surrender charge percentage in Year 15 and beyond?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.2 Surrender Charge Schedule (% of Specified Amount): Year 15+: 0%

qa_pairs.question: If the policy owner skips premium payments and the account value becomes insufficient to cover monthly deductions, what is the maximum number of days coverage continues before the policy lapses?
qa_pairs.answer: 61 days, which is the grace period that begins when the account value is insufficient.
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.3 You may skip payments as long as the Account Value is sufficient to cover monthly deductions. / 2.4 If Account Value falls below the amount needed to pay monthly charges, the policy will enter a 61-day grace period. / 10.2 If sufficient premium is not received within 61 days, policy lapses.

qa_pairs.question: Under Option A, if the policy has an outstanding loan at the time of the insured's death, what death benefit will be paid?
qa_pairs.answer: Under Option A the death benefit equals the Specified Amount, but it is paid minus any outstanding policy loans.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.1 OPTION A (Level Death Benefit): Death Benefit = Specified Amount / 5.4 Death benefit is paid minus any outstanding policy loans.

qa_pairs.question: Can the policy owner make a partial withdrawal of $300?
qa_pairs.answer: No. The minimum partial withdrawal amount is $500.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.2 Minimum withdrawal: $500

qa_pairs.evidence: N/A

qa_pairs.evidence: N/A

qa_pairs.evidence: N/A
